text:
IRS Chief Frank Bisignano told lawmakers the agency is staying tough on some of the wealthiest taxpayers as Democrats pressed him over steep workforce cuts last year.

Read more at the link in our bio.

📸 : Anna Moneymaker/Getty Images

Minnesota lawmakers are weighing legislation imposing what backers say would be the nation’s first true state-level wealth tax—a 1% annual levy on all “taxable wealth” above $10 million.

Read more at the link in our bio.

📸 : Henryk Sadura/Getty Images

Initiatives to regulate and tax prediction markets are accelerating in the closing weeks of state legislative sessions, with many lawmakers denouncing the fast-growing trading platforms as a form of unlicensed and unregulated gambling.

Read more at the link in our bio.

📸 : Samuel Boivin/NurPhoto via Getty

President Donald Trump proposed a cut of $1.4 billion in annual funding for the IRS, in his fiscal 2027 budget proposal released Friday.

The cut come as the IRS recovers from steep workforce losses and leadership turnover in 2025 and as it prepares to finish up a high stakes tax season.

Learn more at the link in our bio. 

📸 Tasos Katopodis/Getty Images

The IRS is undertaking its first full filing season since DOGE slashed a quarter of the agency’s 100,000-person staff last year as part of Trump’s promise to reshape the federal workforce and root out waste and fraud. But IRS employees share multiple challenges—including a backlog of tax returns, a shortage of employees, and human resource and IT workers forced to process returns.

Read more at the link in our bio.

📸 :  Tasos Katopodis/Getty Images; Samuel Corum/Getty Images; Michael Bocchieri/Getty Images

Many Americans work with tax experts and money managers to navigate IRS rules and keep as much of their income as legally allowed. But those with a higher tolerance for risk can easily find promoters to recommend newer, often untested strategies that promise bigger savings.
 
Learn more at the link in our bio.

Tax-sheltering strategies, whether legitimate or illegal, grow out of real or perceived gaps in the Internal Revenue Code. Promoters pore through the code to find exceptions and exclusions and build shelter structures designed to exploit the gaps.
 
Swipe to learn more about the process and visit the link in our bio to read about one company and its strategy.

High-profile retirements and competitive elections among senior tax writers will likely give the House Ways and Means Committee a new look next Congress.

Read more at the link in our bio.

📸 : Daniel Heuer/Bloomberg

Increasing acrimony against the Trump administration over its trade, defense, and tax policies will make it difficult for countries to agree on how to tax big tech companies.

Read more at the link in our bio.

📸 : Roberto Schmidt/Getty Images

The statehouse debates over whether to end or cut property taxes bills come amid rising costs and sticker shock for first-time buyers and seniors on fixed incomes. Georgia, Florida, Texas, and Ohio are among the states grappling with the issue.

Read more at the link in our bio.

Facing a shortage of accountants, state accounting boards are vigorously trying to attract younger workers to the profession through outreach like a rock-music themed, college tour to publicize easing of decades old licensing requirements.

Learn more at the link in our bio.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, who has been acting IRS commissioner since August, is setting up the IRS for potential legal risks the longer he stays in the spot temporarily.
🔗 Read more at the link in our bio.
📸 : Graeme Sloan/Bloomberg
